title: Exact and Approximate Nearest-Neighbor Indexes
description: An HNSW index is called exact, its recall is measured against itself, tenant filters are applied after top-k, and memory or build cost is absent from the decision. Learners cumulatively produce ANN recall, latency, and resource experiment through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.
Explain and tune HNSW construction, search breadth, graph degree, recall, latency, memory, updates, and deletion behavior.
Explain coarse partitioning, probes, product or scalar quantization, training data, compression loss, and rebuild requirements.
